title: 100,000-year-old Homo sapiens bones from Ethiopia may preserve earliest evidence of human cremation - Archaeology News Online Magazine
description: 100,000-year-old Homo sapiens bones from Ethiopia may preserve earliest evidence of human cremation Archaeology News Online Magazine 100,000-year-old burned bones at famed Afar Rift might be the oldest cremation on Earth Interesting Engineering Evidence Found in Ethiopia That Homo sapiens Cremated Their Dead 100,000 Years Ago, 60,000 Before the Oldest Known Record La Brújula Verde The first signs of human cremation may date back 100,000 years Phys.org

title: What is freekeh? The high-protein, fiber-rich ancient grain that’s good for your brain and metabolism
description: The nutritional profile of freekeh, an ancient Middle Eastern grain, packs a punch, rivaling even powerhouse carbohydrates like quinoa.

title: Gig review: Ancient Infinity Orchestra at Leeds City Varieties
